title: Juncus × kern-reichgeltii
taxonomy: Juncus × kern-reichgeltii is a hybrid of two Juncus species. It belongs to the family Juncaceae and is a member of the genus Juncus. As a hybrid, its classification may not be as clear-cut as that of its parent species. The Juncus × kern-reichgeltii is often classified as a nothospecies, which is a hybrid species that is not readily assigned to one of the parent species. The exact classification may vary depending on the source and the specific characteristics of the plant.

intro: Juncus × kern-reichgeltii, also known as Kern-Reichgelt rush or Kern-Reichgelt's rush, is a hybrid species of flowering plant in the family Juncaceae, characterized by its slender stems and small, brownish-green flowers. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various conditions, such as wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Juncus × kern-reichgeltii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and phenolic glycosides, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Juncus × kern-reichgeltii is commonly prepared as a tincture, infusion, or decoction, which can be used topically to soothe skin irritations and internally to support immune function.
